channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: City of London special constable and former Hong Kong police officer on trial for spying for China
item.description: A trial has begun at the Old Bailey in London involving two men accused of breaching the UK's National Security Act by assisting a foreign intelligence service linked to Hong Kong and China. Chi Leung...

item.title: Zanzibar government vows to restrict firearm use by Special Unit amid complaints
item.description: Zanzibar government has recognised public complaints about the Special Unit forces using violence and confiscating goods at Darajani Main Market. During a press briefing on the 100-day achievements of...

item.title: Philippines coordinates with Portugal for the extradition of Zaldy Co
item.description: The Philippine government, under President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., is actively coordinating with Portuguese authorities to facilitate the return of Zaldy Co. Manila has issued a red notice through Inter...

item.title: NADRA Updates Records by Cancelling CNICs of Deceased Citizens
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Nadra has taken a major step to ensure the accuracy of Pakistan’s national identity records by cancelling approximately 4.2 million CNICs of individuals who are recorded as deceased in prov...

item.title: New Technologies Offer Hope for Sleep Apnea Sufferers
item.description: For decades, sleep apnea treatment has largely relied on cumbersome CPAP machines. However, a new wave of technological advancements is expanding treatment options beyond this one-size-fits-all soluti...

item.title: Experts Recommend Starting Colorectal Cancer Screening at Age 45
item.description: The Canadian Cancer Society is urging provincial and territorial governments to lower the routine screening age for colorectal cancer from 50 to 45. This recommendation comes in response to rising rat...

item.title: Duterte's Explicit Calls to Kill Linked to Drug War Deaths at ICC Pre-Trial
item.description: This opinion piece by Patricia Evangelista, published on March 11, 2026, examines the confirmation of charges hearing at the International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague against former President Ro...

item.title: FG holds dialogue with foreign investors to improve Nigeria’s business climate
item.description: For Abuja on Thursday, the Federal Government, through the Presidential Enabling Business Environment Council (PEBEC), organize the Third Existing Foreign Direct Investors (FDI) Roundtable to tackle c...

item.title: Iranian Women's Football Team Members Seek Asylum in Australia Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Seven members of the Iranian women's football team have sought asylum in Australia following the Women's Asian Cup tournament, after being labelled 'traitors' by Iranian state media for refusing to si...

item.title: Nairobi Launches Digital LiquorPay Platform, Boosts Licence Issuance
item.description: Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja has praised the LiquorPay digital system, which allows liquor businesses to apply and pay for licences online. Launched barely a month ago, the platform has already led...

item.title: San Pedro College-Davao City Graduate Achieves Highest Score in March 2026 Medical Technologists Licensure Exam
item.description: A graduate from San Pedro College in Davao City emerged as the top performer in the recent licensure examination for medical technologists conducted by the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC). Th...

item.title: Common Gut Symptoms That Could Signal Bigger Health Issues
item.description: Many people in Tanzania tend to ignore digestive problems thinking they are just normal day-to-day issues. Symptoms like bloating, persistent heartburn, constipation, fatigue, and difficulty concentra...

item.title: Amit Shah Responds to Rahul Gandhi on Speaker's Conduct During Budget Session
item.description: During the recent Parliament Budget Session, there was a tense exchange between Union Home Minister Amit Shah and Congress leader Rahul Gandhi regarding the conduct of the Speaker. Rahul Gandhi had cr...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Health Workers’ Skills on Proper Use of Medical Equipment
item.description: The Tanzanian government is continuing to focus on improving maternal and child health services by training health workers on proper use of medical equipment. The Ministry of Health organised a traini...

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Conflict: Survival Over Victory
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran has rapidly escalated, spanning multiple fronts including missile strikes, attacks on US military infrastructure, and clashes involving...

item.title: High Court rules Kneecap member cannot face terror trial due to timing error
item.description: The UK government has been unsuccessful in its appeal over a terror charge against Liam Óg Ó hAnnaidh, also known as Mo Chara, a member of the Irish language rap group Kneecap. Ó hAnnaidh had been acc...

item.title: Eastern Ontario and Western Quebec Schools and Services Disrupted by Freezing Rain
item.description: A major freezing rainstorm has caused widespread disruptions across eastern Ontario and western Quebec, prompting school closures and bus cancellations. On Wednesday, nearly all school buses were susp...

item.title: Remarkable Scientists Who Tested Themselves and Pushed Boundaries
item.description: Science is often portrayed as a pristine, orderly pursuit with strict protocols and ethical committees. Yet history shows a far more human and daring side, where curiosity, obsession, and bravery—some...

item.title: Kenya and South Africa Strengthen Historic Ties Through Trade and Diplomacy
item.description: The diplomatic and economic relationship between Kenya and South Africa has deep roots in the shared history of Africa's liberation movements. Speaking at the Global South Africans Fireside Chat in Na...

item.title: Durham Police Offer $15,000 Reward in Oshawa Homicide Case as Family Seeks Answers
item.description: Durham Regional Police have announced a $15,000 reward for information that could lead to the arrest and conviction of those responsible for a recent homicide in Oshawa. The investigation follows the ...
